author: claude-code-agent

patch.value: TypeScript-first extension & package scaffolding + SDK docs reframe

Honor ADR pm-2c28 (extensions must be authored fully in TypeScript) by converting the pm extension/package init scaffolder to emit TypeScript source instead of plain .js, and reframing the SDK authoring docs/docstrings TypeScript-first. Today the scaffolder emits index.js with a single JSDoc @param annotation while every first-party package is TS (index.ts compiled to index.js). Scaffold index.ts + tsconfig.json + a build script + colocated index.test.ts, with the manifest entry pointing at the compiled ./index.js and the README documenting the install+build flow; update scaffold tests, smoke:external-packages, and dogfood:package-first to build the TS scaffold before loading. Reframe docs/SDK.md + docs/EXTENSIONS.md and the define*/compose* docstrings to justify the helpers by their TypeScript value (literal-type preservation + handler inference) rather than 'JS cannot annotate'.

message: Track TypeScript-first scaffolding + SDK docs reframe feature

value.author: codex
value.text: Evidence: PR #352 review follow-up implemented. Fixed mergeExtensionBlueprints teardown to run every deactivate in LIFO order and rethrow the first failure; made TypeScript scaffolds compile recursive **/*.ts modules; changed package scaffold .gitignore to root wildcard compiled outputs; added @types/node to standalone README and runtime next-step install commands; hardened dogfood spawnSync failure formatting for null output streams. Verification passed: linked pm test command (4 focused files, 213 tests) passed in PM sandbox; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed literal 100/100/100/100 (31041 statements, 24166 branches, 5289 functions, 29791 lines; 4987 tests); pnpm release:gates passed after latest source change with the same 100/100/100/100 coverage, npx packed smoke, and dogfood:package-first temp project (88 commands); focused manual temp smoke passed for package init + extension init, recursive subdir helper.ts emitting src/helper.js, wildcard .gitignore, README @types guidance, and next_steps @types guidance; pnpm changelog:pm:check passed with pm-changelog 2026.6.20 and CHANGELOG.md unchanged; pnpm audit reported no vulnerabilities; pnpm outdated only reported @sentry/node 10.59.0 -> 10.60.0, @types/node 20.19.43 -> 26.0.0, commander 14.0.3 -> 15.0.0, not bundled into this review-fix PR due semver/runtime risk; pm package doctor and pm health were ok; pm validate history_drift ok with existing resolution metadata warnings; GH issue #348 remains tracked in pm-2kkl as intermittent Windows nightly worker teardown, while current PR Windows regression checks pass.

value.author: codex
value.text: Evidence update: addressed fresh PR review feedback on current head. SDK mergeExtensionBlueprints now drives hook merging from one canonical hook-key list while preserving typed per-kind assignment and empty-surface omission; the existing merge test now covers before_command, after_command, on_write, on_read, and on_index. Package scaffolds now ignore compiled TypeScript output recursively with *.js and *.test.js, matching recursive tsconfig include, and scaffold/runtime tests assert the new behavior. Verification: focused linked PM test passed in schema sandbox (4 files, 213 tests); direct node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed with literal 100/100/100/100 (31059/31059 statements, 24182/24182 branches, 5289/5289 functions, 29809/29809 lines); direct pnpm release:gates passed including docs/skills, static quality, version policy, security scan, packed npx smoke, telemetry best-effort, and package-first dogfood (88 commands; semantic dogfood skipped because PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC not set); pnpm changelog:pm:check passed with pm-changelog 2026.6.20 and CHANGELOG.md unchanged; manual temp-dir smoke passed at /tmp/tmp.qmmsLY53Di with isolated PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH, package init + extension init, npm install/build, root index.js, subdir src/helper.js, recursive gitignore patterns, and @types/node guidance in README and next_steps. GitHub/dependency/security checks: PR #352 exists on feat/ts-first-modular-extension-authoring; issue #348 remains the known Windows nightly flake tracked separately as pm-2kkl; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate found no vulnerabilities; outdated packages remain @sentry/node 10.59.0->10.60.0, @types/node 20.19.43->26.0.0, commander 14.0.3->15.0.0 and were not mixed into this SDK/scaffold PR due runtime/semver scope. Linked-test note: release:gates, changelog check, and full coverage were verified directly in the real repo because the PM linked-test schema sandbox intentionally lacks full repo docs/skill fixtures or real tracker state needed by those gates; the linked PM test list now contains only the focused sandbox-safe suite.

value.author: codex
value.text: Evidence update: addressed fresh Gemini review on current head 2cbaf305. mergeExtensionBlueprints now invokes activate/deactivate through the original blueprint object, preserving method this context while keeping activate forward order, deactivate LIFO order, and best-effort teardown error collection. Added SDK regression coverage for this-bound lifecycle methods across merged modules. Verification after this fix: focused suite passed (4 files, 214 tests); node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed literal 100/100/100/100 (31062/31062 statements, 24182/24182 branches, 5289/5289 functions, 29812/29812 lines; 4988 tests); pnpm release:gates passed with docs/skills, static quality, version check, security scan, npx packed smoke, telemetry best-effort, and package-first dogfood (88 commands; semantic dogfood skipped because PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC not set); PM-linked focused test passed in schema sandbox; pnpm changelog:pm:check passed with pm-changelog 2026.6.20 and CHANGELOG.md unchanged.
